title: Aluminum rose to 3,478 USD/T on April 8, 2026
summary: Aluminum prices rose to $3,478 per tonne, up 0.39% from the previous day. Over the past month, prices have increased 2.76%, and are up 48.47% compared to the same time last year. Aluminum futures in the UK approached their highest level in more than four years, with market caution driven by geopolitical tensions.

title: Aluminium prices surge over 10% as Iran war disrupts global supply
summary: Aluminium prices are soaring due to supply disruptions across the Gulf region, with key smelters facing shutdowns and exports disrupted via the Strait of Hormuz. LME aluminium futures have surged over 10% since the conflict began, with global inventories hitting multi-month lows and experts warning of a deepening global supply deficit.

title: Aluminum Price Analysis Today - April 7, 2026
summary: Aluminum prices remained flat globally at $3,461.28 per ton, with the primary drivers being Iranian missile strikes that severely damaged major smelters and the Strait of Hormuz closure that paralyzed Gulf exports. Analysts project a 570,000-tonne global deficit, exposing the market to significant upside risk.

title: Scientists at Oak Ridge National Laboratory create new aluminum alloy called RidgeAlloy
summary: Researchers at Oak Ridge National Laboratory have developed a new aluminum alloy called RidgeAlloy that can process contaminated car materials. The innovation represents a significant advancement in aluminum recycling and material science technology.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices rose to 3,092 CNY/T on April 8, 2026, up 0.13% from the previous day, despite a 0.83% decline over the past month. Steel rebar futures held below CNY 3,090 per ton at one-month lows following the temporary reopening of the Strait of Hormuz amid a US-Iran ceasefire. Prices face pressure from demand concerns, policy uncertainty, and potential US tariff revisions on steel products.

title: Iran steel supply unlikely to benefit from ceasefire
summary: Iran's steel production is unlikely to recover quickly despite a two-week US-Iran ceasefire that may ease energy prices and resume shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Damage to major mills could keep slab and billet prices elevated in Asia and other markets, with fob Asia slab prices up $24/t since late February. The ceasefire has led to sharp drops in European gas and Brent crude prices, potentially lowering production costs, though no immediate impact on steel prices was observed.

title: SMU Price Ranges: Sheet and plate continue to inch higher on tight spot market and despite Iran War concerns
summary: Sheet and plate prices held steady or ticked upward due to limited spot tonnage, stable demand, limited import competition, and domestic mill outages. Hot-rolled coil prices are expected to continue climbing, with many predicting they will hit or exceed $1,100 per short ton. April scrap prices are anticipated to decline $20-$30 per gross ton, except for prime scrap which remains sideways.

title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: HRC Steel fell slightly to 1,078.06 USD/T on April 7, 2026, down 0.18%, but has risen 3.96% over the past month and 17.18% year-over-year. Prices recently reached 1,075.00 USD/T, the highest since January 2024, and gained 2.8% over the past four weeks.

title: AISI: Raw steel production jumps to 20-week high
summary: US raw steel production reached 1,778,000 short tons for the week ending January 24, marking a 1.3% increase from the prior week and the highest in 20 weeks. Year-to-date production stands at 6,016,000 short tons, up 3.4% from last year, with capability utilization at 76.9%. Production rose in most regions, including the South and Great Lakes.

title: Industry Data
summary: Domestic raw steel production was 1,831,000 net tons for the week ending April 4, 2026, with a capability utilization rate of 79.1%, up 1.6% from the previous week and 8.2% from the same week last year. Adjusted year-to-date production through April 4 reached 24,028,000 net tons, a 5.2% increase year-over-year at 77.4% utilization. Regional production included strong output from the Southern district at 826,000 net tons.

title: World Steel News
summary: Meranti Green Steel reaffirmed its 2030 production timeline for a high value-added steel project in Duqm, focusing on expanding overall capacity. The update highlights ongoing plans for green steel production amid global industry developments.

title: New technology could cut steel production costs 20%, Hertha CEO says
summary: Hertha Metals has developed a technology that replaces traditional coal-based steelmaking, producing steel over 20% cheaper with 50% fewer emissions using any grade of domestic iron ore. The company plans to break ground on a 10,000 metric ton/year plant in July next to its Texas pilot, aiming for operations by end of next year. CEO Laureen Meroueh highlighted its role in enhancing competitiveness and sustainability in primary steelmaking.

title: ResponsibleSteel and IRMA publish landmark report on just transitions in mining and steel
summary: ResponsibleSteel and the Initiative for Responsible Mining Assurance (IRMA) released a report outlining how voluntary sustainability standards can support fair and inclusive transitions as mining and steel sectors decarbonize. It is the first such report jointly authored by standards from both sectors. The organization also advances interoperability for global alignment on steel decarbonization and integrates renewable energy in steelmaking.

title: Fives Group and Hyundai Steel Sign Contract for Advanced Coil Finishing Lines
summary: Fives Group signed a contract with Hyundai Steel to design and supply advanced coil finishing lines for automotive steel at a new U.S. flat steel mill by Hyundai-POSCO Louisiana Steel. This supports innovative production technologies for high-quality automotive applications. The development aligns with advancements in steel processing efficiency.

title: Mesabi Metallics Secures US$150 Million for DR-Grade Iron Ore Mine and Pellet Plant
summary: Mesabi Metallics, along with Essar Group, secured US$150 million from Macquarie Group to start a direct reduction (DR)-grade iron ore mine and pellet plant in Nashwauk, Minn., in Q3 2026. DR-grade pellets enable lower-emission steel production via direct reduced iron processes. This financing boosts sustainable domestic iron supply for green steelmaking.

title: Rio Tinto's Nuton technology produces first copper
summary: Rio Tinto's Nuton technology has produced its first copper at the Johnson Camp mine in Arizona, achieving the lowest mine-to-metal carbon footprint in the U.S. at 0.82 kg CO₂-e per kg copper. The technology enables cleaner, faster production from low-grade ores, using up to 80% less water and 60% lower emissions than traditional methods, while extending mine life. It reached industrial scale in just 18 months, far quicker than the industry average.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel futures rose to around $17,300 per tonne, rebounding from a recent over one-month low, supported by Indonesia’s reduced production quotas for 2026 and hopes for Middle East de-escalation. Prices have stabilized in the $17,000–$17,400 range amid elevated global inventories and projected surplus, with subdued demand from stainless steel and batteries. Western Australia introduced interest-free loans in April to aid nickel miners.

title: Live Nickel Prices Today
summary: Nickel prices advanced 1.47% to $17,347.34 per ton globally and ¥118,431 per ton in China, driven by Indonesia's reduced ore production quotas alleviating oversupply fears and recovering demand from Chinese EV batteries and stainless steel. However, upside is capped by tiered Indonesian royalty rates at higher prices, keeping the market range-bound and policy-driven.
